Since net income is a component of ROIC, it is not possible for the company to report net income of $241 million (as stated in sentence [16]) and have an ROIC of 13.2% (as stated in sentence [31]) without also reporting total assets and total equity. ROIC = Net Income / (Total Assets - Total Equity)

(Total Assets - Total Equity) = $241 million / 13.2%
